name: Chocolate-Orange Guinness Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F.
2. Grease two 8-9-inch cake pans.
3. Cream the butter and sugar until light and fluffy.
4. Sift flour, baking powder, salt, and cocoa into a bowl.
5. Add orange rind to the creamed mixture; beat in eggs one at a time, incorporating a spoonful of the dry ingredients with each egg.
6. Gently mix in Guinness, tablespoon by tablespoon, adding more dry ingredients as needed.
7. If any flour remains, fold it in gently. Mix thoroughly without over-beating.
8. Divide batter between pans and smooth tops.
9. Bake for 35-40 minutes at moderate temperature (350°F), until cakes are springy to the touch and slightly firm in the pans.
10. Turn out onto wire racks to cool completely.
Meanwhile, make the icing:
1. Cream butter and confectioners' sugar until smooth.
2. Blend in grated orange rind and a small amount of juice for an easily spreadable consistency.
3. Once cakes are cooled, sandwich them together with half the icing; spread remaining icing on top.
